alt_forms.note: Deployment/placement nuance: 配置 emphasizes the act of placing or arranging units, while 位置 refers to a location or positional state.

definitions.gloss_en: the spatial location of a unit on the battlefield; a unit's position
definitions.gloss_jp: {ユニット|ゆにっと}が{戦|せん}{場|じょう}の{どこ|どこ}にいるかを{示|しめ}す{空|くう}{間|かん}{的|てき}{座|ざ}{標|ひょう}
definitions.explanation_en: A unit's current spatial location on the playing surface. Used when referring to where something is, as opposed to the act of placing it (配置).
definitions.implicit_notes: 位置 is a general-purpose Japanese word for location/position, not game-specific. 配置 (haichi) is the related concept of arrangement or deployment.
definitions.orthography_notes: 位置: 位 (rank/place) + 置 (put/place). Standard dictionary word. JWC documentation and Fire Emblem both use 配置 for unit placement in the setup phase, and 位置 for positional state during play.
definitions.game_setting_notes: In miniature wargames: 位置 refers to a unit's location at any given moment. 配置 is used specifically at the start of the game for deploying units. Both are generic and not WH-specific.

usage_jp: {ユニット|ゆにっと}の{位|い}{置|ち}を{確|かく}{認|にん}する (check a unit's position); {配|はい}{置|ち}する (to deploy/place)
usage_en: "position" for state; "placement" or "deployment" for 配置.
comparative_notes: 配置 (haichi) implies active arrangement by a player. 位置 is the resulting state. Both are needed in a wargame rulebook.

examples.japanese: {位|い}{置|ち}について、よーい、ドン！
examples.english: On your mark, get set, go!

cross_references.note: 戦場 — the battlefield within which 位置 is defined
射線 — line of sight, affected by position
